title: Armmersive
description: Ultra lightweight, client-side first person armor/damage overlay and visibility configuration
body:
# Armmersive

Ultra lightweight, client-side first person armor/damage overlay and visibility configuration

[![Badge showing the amount of downloads on modrinth](https://img.shields.io/badge/dynamic/json?color=2d2d2d&colorA=5da545&label=&suffix=%20downloads%20&query=downloads&url=https://api.modrinth.com/v2/project/XOSo49tE&style=flat-square&logo=modrinth&logoColor=2d2d2d)](https://modrinth.com/mod/armmersive)
[![Badge showing the amount of downloads on curseforge](https://cf.way2muchnoise.eu/full_1362712_downloads.svg?badge_style=flat)](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/armmersive)
[![Badge linking to issues on github](https://img.shields.io/badge/dynamic/json?query=value&url=https%3A%2F%2Fimg.shields.io%2Fgithub%2Fissues-raw%2Fchyzman%2Farmmersive.json&label=&logo=github&color=2d2d2d&style=flat-square&labelColor=6e5494&logoColor=2d2d2d&suffix=%20issues)](https://github.com/chyzman/armmersive/issues)

Armmersive is a client-sided mod that adds a handful of features to enhance your first person arms. It's ultra lightweight, zero performance impact, no dependencies and is fully configurable in skin settings or with mod menu.

## Features
1. Armor rendering on your first person arms (toggleable)
    - works with any armor, modded included and supports enchantments, trims and dyes
2. Damage overlay on your first person arms (toggleable)
    - carries over the red tint overlay when taking damage
3. Choose which of your off/main hand are visible when (always/full/never)
4. Full compatibility with [WearThat](https://modrinth.com/mod/wearthat), one of my other mods
